   "reasoning": "Honestly, I think the most crucial thing here is injecting some funds into the system to prevent total collapse. With class sizes ballooning and teachers burning out, it's clear that the system's being stretched too thin. I'd love to see some of those funds go directly to keeping our (already overworked) teachers on board, but I'm not convinced scholarships are the way to go - we've got enough smart kids who work hard without needing handouts. And please, for the love of all things good, give us some transparency on which schools are actually doing better than others.",
   "confidence": 0.85
}
